module_title: Stressors Affecting Abuse and Neglect Across the Lifespan
learning_objectives: Identify clinical indicators of abuse across lifespan.
Develop immediate and long-term safety plans for abuse survivors.
key_focus_areas: Legal and ethical responsibilities in abuse situations.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.content: Abuse and neglect occur at any age and often lead to lasting trauma. This overview defines key terms and sets the stage for screening.

sections.title: Physical Abuse Indicators
sections.content: Unexplained bruises, burns, or fractures may signal harm. Injuries at different healing stages warrant further questioning.

sections.title: Sexual Abuse Indicators
sections.content: Sexual abuse includes any non‑consensual act. Look for genital injuries, sexually transmitted infections, or fearful behavior.

sections.title: Emotional Abuse and Neglect
sections.content: Belittling, threats, and withholding basic needs erode self‑worth. Victims may appear anxious, depressed, or withdrawn.

sections.title: Vulnerable Populations
sections.content: Children, partners, and elders each present unique warning signs. Nurses consider developmental level and dependency.

sections.title: Cycle of Violence
sections.content: Tension building, acute violence, and reconciliation create repeating patterns that trap many survivors.

sections.title: Assessment and Documentation
sections.content: Use trauma‑informed questions, document objectively, and employ screening tools to uncover hidden abuse.

sections.title: Mandatory Reporting
sections.content: Laws require nurses to report certain abuse. Know local statutes and follow organizational policies.

sections.title: Safety Planning and Referrals
sections.content: Help survivors develop escape plans and link them with shelters, counseling, and legal resources.

sections.title: Nurse Self‑Care
sections.content: Caring for abuse survivors can be emotionally taxing. Seek supervision and practice self‑care to avoid burnout.
